title: Teaching
text:
I enjoy supporting rigorous training in microeconomics and contributing to methods-oriented instruction for students at different stages of study.






Bocconi University | Undergraduate level | Teaching Assistant | 2024 and 2025
Teaching support for core undergraduate microeconomics.



Bocconi University | PhD level | Teaching Assistant
Teaching support for doctoral-level microeconomics training.








IGC Research Methodology Workshop | August 2025
Guest lecture on advanced approaches to causal inference.

title: Home
text:
Economics PhD Candidate


I am a third-year PhD student in Economics at Bocconi University. My research sits at the intersection of development and labour economics, with a focus on how institutions and social protection shape labour market outcomes.
I also work with Bayesian methods in applied microeconomics and meta-analysis, building on previous research experience at Bocconi University and the Global Poverty Research Lab at Northwestern University.

Research Download CV Email

title: Home
text:
Selected Work
A snapshot of current research on workplace institutions, social protection, and evidence synthesis.


Union Delegates Incentives and Firm-level Outcomes
with Francesco Armillei | Working paper
A study of how the electoral accountability of workplace union representatives affects employment outcomes, bargaining content, and within-firm inequality.

Working papers



Unconditional Cash Transfers: A Bayesian Meta-Analysis of 50 Randomized Evaluations in 26 Low- and Middle-Income Countries
Working paper
A Bayesian meta-analysis of unconditional cash transfer programs that evaluates average impacts and tests key policy mechanisms across settings.

Press coverage

title: Home
text:
Research Themes


Development Economics
Social protection, household welfare, and the design of policies that shape economic opportunity in low- and middle-income countries.


Labour Economics
Institutions, workplace representation, and the ways bargaining structures affect workers and firms.


Bayesian Methods
Applications of Bayesian statistics to applied microeconomics, meta-analysis, and evidence synthesis.

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Curriculum Vitae

A current copy of my CV is available below. The PDF download is the best option for mobile browsers and for sharing a direct copy.


Download CV (PDF) Email


If the embedded viewer does not load in your browser, use the PDF download above.

title: Research
text:
My research focuses on development and labour economics, with a particular interest in institutional design, social protection, and Bayesian evidence synthesis in applied microeconomics.



I study how policies and institutions shape worker welfare, firm behavior, and the distribution of economic opportunity. Across projects, I combine applied microeconomic questions with quantitative methods that strengthen causal inference and improve evidence aggregation.






with Francesco Armillei | Working paper
We study how electoral accountability for workplace union representatives affects firm-level employment outcomes after a 2008 reform in France tied union legitimacy to vote shares and approval thresholds. The project examines labor earnings, hours worked, bargaining content, and within-firm inequality.



Working paper
Using Bayesian meta-analysis, this project aggregates evidence from 115 studies of 72 unconditional cash transfer programs and evaluates how impacts vary across policy-relevant mechanisms and contexts.

Ideas for India VoxDev







Selected external coverage of the cash transfer meta-analysis includes Ideas for India and VoxDev.
